title: READABILITY: Document orthogonal test purposes in message delivery tests
description: relay/tests/e2e_jsonrpc.rs:75-194 — Two tests (two_client_message_delivery and from_field_is_alice_pub_id) share identical setup boilerplate (spawn relay, generate identities, wait for DirectoryList). The test docstrings explain what each verifies but not why both are necessary or why they're separate tests. A reader might think: 'These could be combined.' The answer is that payload integrity and sender identity verification are orthogonal concerns: one proves the payload bytes survive relay forwarding; the other proves the relay sets sender identity from auth, not from untrusted client fields. Fix: add a comment above from_field_is_alice_pub_id explaining 'This test verifies a separate concern from two_client_message_delivery: that sender identity (from field) is relay-authenticated, not client-provided. Payload and sender identity are independent axes and must be tested separately.'
close_reason: P4 readability/comment items. Not blocking correctness. Deferred — file separately if prioritized.
